title: ⭐⭐ Multi-select: check ALL the correct answers

questions.prompt: Among these numbers, check ALL that are multiples of 3.
questions.explanation: 9 = 3 × 3 and 21 = 3 × 7 are multiples of 3. 10 and 16 are not (10 ÷ 3 and 16 ÷ 3 are not whole numbers).

questions.prompt: About an even number, check ALL the statements that are true.

options.text: it must be a multiple of 4
its sum with another even number is always even

questions.explanation: An even number is indeed divisible by 2 (a) and ends in 0/2/4/6/8 (b); the sum of two even numbers is always even (d). But 6 is even without being a multiple of 4, so c is false.

questions.prompt: Which of these sequences advance by a CONSTANT step (an arithmetic progression)? Check ALL that apply.
questions.explanation: 3, 6, 9, 12 advances by +3 each step, and 5, 8, 11, 14 also advances by +3: both are arithmetic progressions. 2, 4, 8, 16 doubles each time (×2, not a constant gap), and 1, 4, 9, 16 are consecutive squares (1², 2², 3², 4²) — neither has a constant step.
